_comment: Content-generation safety library for worksheet batches. The seed script ALWAYS injects `global.required` and `global.excluded` into every prompt, and appends `themes.{ref}.required` / `themes.{ref}.excluded` whenever a topic config sets `safety_ref`. The same `excluded` arrays double as the human-reviewer rejection checklist — see docs/features/CONTENT_GENERATION_SAFETY.md.

required: cute cartoon kid-safe illustrations
bright friendly colors
age-appropriate for children
clean educational layout
excluded: graffiti with any text or profanity
ambiguous body proportions for characters labeled as kids

hiphop.description: Hip hop music theme for kids. Celebrates the positive, creative, community side of hip hop — DJs, breakdancers, beatboxers, rappers, graffiti letters of the alphabet, vinyl records, headphones, turntables. NOT streetwear edginess, grills, gang culture, or anything provocative.
required: kid-friendly hip hop imagery
cute cartoon kids as DJs, breakdancers, beatboxers, or young rappers holding a toy microphone
bright playful colors
clean block-letter graffiti that only spells the educational skill (numbers, alphabet, the topic name)
age-appropriate streetwear: t-shirts, jeans, sneakers, baseball caps turned forward or sideways
excluded: alcohol bottles, red cups, smoke, vapes
twerking, grinding, or any suggestive dancing
baggy clothes falling off, visible underwear, sagging pants
graffiti that spells anything other than the educational topic or the letters/numbers being taught
profanity, slurs, or real-world slang that could be offensive
dark moody urban alleys; prefer bright classroom, stage, park, or studio settings

disability_central.description: Disability representation as central, empowered figures — kids using wheelchairs, crutches, hearing aids, cochlear implants, prosthetic limbs, white canes, or AAC devices are the hero of the worksheet, doing the same activity as peers, not being helped or pitied.
required: child with disability is the main character, smiling and active
mobility aid (wheelchair, crutches, prosthetic, cane, hearing aid, cochlear implant, AAC device) rendered accurately and positively
child is doing the educational activity themselves
excluded: pity framing, sad expressions, being rescued or carried
disability as the 'problem to solve' in the worksheet

emotions.description: Emotional understanding themes — feelings wheels, big-feelings characters, identifying emotions. Must depict the full range (happy, sad, angry, scared, worried, surprised, calm, excited) authentically but age-appropriately.
required: clearly-labeled cartoon faces for each emotion
safe, calm presentation even of 'big' emotions
coping cues alongside difficult emotions (deep breath, hug, water, counting)
excluded: crying that looks distressing or traumatic
fear imagery that could scare a young child (monsters, blood, darkness alone)

holidays_christmas.description: Christmas theme kept broadly festive and inclusive — snow, presents, Santa, reindeer, cookies, wreaths, ornaments.
required: classic festive imagery: Santa, reindeer, snow, tree, wreath, stockings, cookies
diverse children celebrating together
warm cozy colors
excluded: religious iconography (crosses, nativity details) when the topic is not explicitly religious — keep it 'festive Christmas'

dinosaurs.description: Dinosaur theme kept scientifically plausible but cute. T-Rex, Stegosaurus, Triceratops, Brachiosaurus, Pterodactyl, Velociraptor in playful poses.
required: cute cartoon proportions (big head, friendly eyes, rounded teeth)
correct species silhouette (so T-Rex looks like T-Rex)
bright friendly colors
excluded: extinction / doom imagery (asteroid impact, dying dinosaurs) for worksheet subjects

seasons.description: Seasonal scenes (spring, summer, fall, winter) that feel universally recognizable and safe.
required: clear seasonal markers (flowers/rain = spring, sun/beach = summer, leaves/pumpkins = fall, snow/mittens = winter)
diverse kids enjoying the season
appropriate clothing for weather
excluded: sunburn, frostbite, or any weather-related harm
swimwear that could read as revealing — prefer one-piece swimsuits, swim trunks, rash guards
